articleRecords.label: Article 1
articleRecords.title: Article 1
articleRecords.summary: This Law is enacted in accordance with the Constitution for the purposes of protecting the rights and interests on personal information, regulating personal information processing activities, and promoting reasonable use of personal information.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: Cyberspace Administration of China — official republication of the NPC text

articleRecords.label: Article 2
articleRecords.title: Article 2
articleRecords.summary: The personal information of natural persons shall be protected by law.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: Cyberspace Administration of China — official republication of the NPC text

articleRecords.label: Article 3
articleRecords.title: Article 3
articleRecords.summary: This Law shall apply to the processing of personal information of natural persons within the territory of the People's Republic of China.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: Cyberspace Administration of China — official republication of the NPC text

articleRecords.label: Article 4
articleRecords.title: Article 4
articleRecords.summary: "Personal information" refers to various information related to an identified or identifiable natural person recorded electronically or by other means, but does not include anonymized information.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: Cyberspace Administration of China — official republication of the NPC text

articleRecords.label: Article 5
articleRecords.title: Article 5
articleRecords.summary: Personal information shall be processed according to law when it is necessary, with justified reason, and in good faith, and the processing may not involve misguidance, fraud, coercion, and the like.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: Cyberspace Administration of China — official republication of the NPC text

articleRecords.label: Article 6
articleRecords.title: Article 6
articleRecords.summary: Personal information processing shall be based on explicit and reasonable purposes and directly related to those purposes, and shall exert the minimum impacts on the rights and interests of individuals.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: Cyberspace Administration of China — official republication of the NPC text

articleRecords.label: Article 7
articleRecords.title: Article 7
articleRecords.summary: The principles of openness and transparency shall be observed in the processing of personal information, the rules for processing personal information shall be disclosed, and the purposes, means, and scope of processing shall be explicitly indicated.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: Cyberspace Administration of China — official republication of the NPC text

articleRecords.label: Article 8
articleRecords.title: Article 8
articleRecords.summary: The quality of personal information shall be guaranteed in personal information processing, to avoid adverse impacts on the rights and interests of individuals caused by inaccurate and incomplete personal information.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: Cyberspace Administration of China — official republication of the NPC text

articleRecords.label: Article 9
articleRecords.title: Article 9
articleRecords.summary: Personal information processors shall be responsible for their personal information processing activities and take necessary measures to ensure the security of the personal information they process.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: Cyberspace Administration of China — official republication of the NPC text

articleRecords.label: Article 10
articleRecords.title: Article 10
articleRecords.summary: No organization or individual shall illegally collect, use, process, or transmit the personal information of other persons, or illegally trade, provide or disclose the personal information of other…
articleRecords.sourceLabel: Cyberspace Administration of China — official republication of the NPC text

articleRecords.label: Article 11
articleRecords.title: Article 11
articleRecords.summary: The state shall establish and improve the personal information protection system to prevent and punish infringements upon the rights and interests on personal information, strengthen publicity and education on personal information…
articleRecords.sourceLabel: Cyberspace Administration of China — official republication of the NPC text

articleRecords.label: Article 12
articleRecords.title: Article 12
articleRecords.summary: The state will actively engage in the development of international rules on personal information protection, promote the international exchanges and cooperation in personal information…
articleRecords.sourceLabel: Cyberspace Administration of China — official republication of the NPC text

articleRecords.label: Article 13
articleRecords.title: Article 13
articleRecords.summary: A personal information processor can process personal information of an individual only if one of the following circumstances exists:
articleRecords.sourceLabel: Cyberspace Administration of China — official republication of the NPC text

articleRecords.label: Article 14
articleRecords.title: Article 14
articleRecords.summary: Where personal information processing is based on individual consent, the individual consent shall be voluntary, explicit, and fully informed.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: Cyberspace Administration of China — official republication of the NPC text

articleRecords.label: Article 15
articleRecords.title: Article 15
articleRecords.summary: Where personal information processing is based on individual consent, an individual shall have the right to withdraw his consent.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: Cyberspace Administration of China — official republication of the NPC text

articleRecords.label: Article 16
articleRecords.title: Article 16
articleRecords.summary: A personal information processor shall not refuse to provide products or services for an individual on the grounds that the individual withholds his consent for the processing of his personal information or has withdrawn his consent for the processing of personal…
articleRecords.sourceLabel: Cyberspace Administration of China — official republication of the NPC text

articleRecords.label: Article 17
articleRecords.title: Article 17
articleRecords.summary: A personal information processor shall, before processing personal information, truthfully, accurately and fully inform an individual of the following matters in a easy-to-notice manner and in clear and easy-to-understand language:
articleRecords.sourceLabel: Cyberspace Administration of China — official republication of the NPC text

articleRecords.label: Article 18
articleRecords.title: Article 18
articleRecords.summary: When processing personal information, personal information processors are permitted not to inform individuals of the matters specified in the first paragraph of the preceding article where laws or administrative regulations require confidentiality or provide no requirement for…
articleRecords.sourceLabel: Cyberspace Administration of China — official republication of the NPC text

articleRecords.label: Article 19
articleRecords.title: Article 19
articleRecords.summary: Except as otherwise provided by laws and administrative regulations, the storage period of personal information shall be the minimum time necessary to achieve the purpose of processing.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: Cyberspace Administration of China — official republication of the NPC text

articleRecords.label: Article 20
articleRecords.title: Article 20
articleRecords.summary: Where two or more personal information processors jointly determine the purposes and means of processing certain personal information, they shall reach an agreement on their respective rights and obligations in processing the personal information.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: Cyberspace Administration of China — official republication of the NPC text

articleRecords.label: Article 21
articleRecords.title: Article 21
articleRecords.summary: A personal information processor entrusting the processing of certain personal information to a party shall reach an agreement with the entrusted party on the purposes, period and means of…
articleRecords.sourceLabel: Cyberspace Administration of China — official republication of the NPC text

articleRecords.label: Article 22
articleRecords.title: Article 22
articleRecords.summary: Where a personal information processor needs to transfer personal information due to a merger, division, dissolution, or bankruptcy or for other…
articleRecords.sourceLabel: Cyberspace Administration of China — official republication of the NPC text

articleRecords.label: Article 23
articleRecords.title: Article 23
articleRecords.summary: To provide personal information for any other processor, a personal information processor shall inform the individuals of the recipient's name and contact information, the purposes and means of processing and the categories of personal information to be…
articleRecords.sourceLabel: Cyberspace Administration of China — official republication of the NPC text

articleRecords.label: Article 24
articleRecords.title: Article 24
articleRecords.summary: Personal information processors using personal information for automated decision making shall ensure the transparency of the decision making and the fairness and impartiality of the…
articleRecords.sourceLabel: Cyberspace Administration of China — official republication of the NPC text

articleRecords.label: Article 25
articleRecords.title: Article 25
articleRecords.summary: Personal information processors shall not disclose the personal information they process, except where separate consents has been obtained from the individuals.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: Cyberspace Administration of China — official republication of the NPC text

articleRecords.label: Article 26
articleRecords.title: Article 26
articleRecords.summary: Image collection and personal identification equipment in public places shall be installed only when it is necessary for the purpose of maintaining public security, and shall be installed in compliance with the relevant provisions of the state and with prominent reminders.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: Cyberspace Administration of China — official republication of the NPC text

articleRecords.label: Article 27
articleRecords.title: Article 27
articleRecords.summary: A personal information processor may reasonably process the personal information disclosed by an individual himself or other legally disclosed personal information, except where the individual expressly refuses.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: Cyberspace Administration of China — official republication of the NPC text

articleRecords.label: Article 28
articleRecords.title: Article 28
articleRecords.summary: "Sensitive personal information" is personal information that once leaked or illegally used, may easily lead to the infringement of the personal dignity of a natural person or may endanger his personal safety or property, including information such as…

articleRecords.summary: Providers should label pictures, videos, and other generated content in accordance with the “Internet Information Service Deep Synthesis Management Provisions”.

articleRecords.label: Article 13
articleRecords.title: Article 13
articleRecords.summary: Providers should offer service that is secure, stable, and uninterrupted, ensuring that users can use them without any disruptions.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: Cyberspace Administration of China — official Order No. 15 publication

articleRecords.label: Article 14
articleRecords.title: Article 14
articleRecords.summary: Where a provider discovers illegal content, it shall promptly take disposal measures such as stopping generation, stopping transmission, and elimination, take measures such as model optimization training for rectification, and report to the relevant responsible authority.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: Cyberspace Administration of China — official Order No. 15 publication

articleRecords.label: Article 15
articleRecords.title: Article 15
articleRecords.summary: Providers should establish and improve a mechanism for receiving complaints and reports, setting up accessible channels for such complaints and reports.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: Cyberspace Administration of China — official Order No. 15 publication

articleRecords.title: Article 16

articleRecords.sourceLabel: Cyberspace Administration of China — official Order No. 15 publication

articleRecords.label: Article 17
articleRecords.title: Article 17
articleRecords.summary: If a generative artificial intelligence service possesses public opinion attributes or social mobilization capability, it should undergo a security assessment in accordance with relevant national regulations.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: Cyberspace Administration of China — official Order No. 15 publication

articleRecords.label: Article 18
articleRecords.title: Article 18
articleRecords.summary: Users have the right to lodge complaints or reports with the relevant responsible authorities if they find that generative artificial intelligence services do not comply with the laws, regulations, or provisions stipulated in these measures.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: Cyberspace Administration of China — official Order No. 15 publication

articleRecords.label: Article 19
articleRecords.title: Article 19
articleRecords.summary: The relevant responsible departments conduct supervision and inspection of generative artificial intelligence services according to their duties, and the provider shall cooperate in accordance with the law, explain the source, scale, type, labeling rules, algorithm…
articleRecords.sourceLabel: Cyberspace Administration of China — official Order No. 15 publication

articleRecords.label: Article 20

articleRecords.summary: For those providing generative artificial intelligence services from outside the territory of the People's Republic of China that do not comply with laws, administrative regulations, and these…
articleRecords.sourceLabel: Cyberspace Administration of China — official Order No. 15 publication

articleRecords.label: Article 21

articleRecords.summary: If providers violate the provisions of these measures, the relevant responsible authorities shall impose penalties in accordance with the "Cybersecurity Law of the People's Republic of China," "Data Security Law of the People's Republic of China," "Personal Information…
articleRecords.sourceLabel: Cyberspace Administration of China — official Order No. 15 publication

articleRecords.label: Article 22
articleRecords.title: Article 22
articleRecords.summary: The meanings of the following terms in these measures are:
articleRecords.sourceLabel: Cyberspace Administration of China — official Order No. 15 publication

articleRecords.label: Article 23
articleRecords.title: Article 23
articleRecords.summary: If laws and administrative regulations stipulate that providing generative artificial intelligence services requires obtaining relevant administrative licenses, providers must obtain the necessary permits in accordance with the law.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: Cyberspace Administration of China — official Order No. 15 publication

articleRecords.label: Article 24
articleRecords.title: Article 24
articleRecords.summary: These measures shall come into effect on August 15, 2023.

articleRecords.label: Article 1
articleRecords.title: Article 1
articleRecords.summary: This Act is enacted to build a smart nation; promote the human-centric research, development, and industrial advancement of artificial intelligence (AI); establish a safe environment for AI applications; ensure digital equity; safeguard the fundamental rights of the…
articleRecords.sourceLabel: National Science and Technology Council — official Traditional Chinese text

articleRecords.label: Article 2
articleRecords.title: Article 2
articleRecords.summary: For the purposes of this Act, the term "competent authority" as used in this Act refers to the National Science and Technology Council at the central government level, and the special municipality, county, or city governments at the local level.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: National Science and Technology Council — official Traditional Chinese text

articleRecords.label: Article 3
articleRecords.title: Article 3
articleRecords.summary: The term "artificial intelligence" as used in this Act refers to a system capable of autonomous operation, which, through input or sensing and by means of machine learning and algorithms, can generate outputs such as predictions, content,…
articleRecords.sourceLabel: National Science and Technology Council — official Traditional Chinese text

articleRecords.label: Article 4
articleRecords.title: Article 4
articleRecords.summary: In promoting the research, development, and application of AI, the government shall, while balancing public interest, digital equity, the promotion of innovation and R&D, and the enhancement of national competitiveness, develop sound governance and…
articleRecords.sourceLabel: National Science and Technology Council — official Traditional Chinese text

articleRecords.label: Article 5
articleRecords.title: Article 5
articleRecords.summary: The government shall ensure that the application of artificial intelligence does not result in any of the following: infringement upon the people's life, body, liberty, or property; disruption of social order, national security, or the ecological…
articleRecords.sourceLabel: National Science and Technology Council — official Traditional Chinese text

articleRecords.label: Article 6
articleRecords.title: Article 6
articleRecords.summary: The Executive Yuan shall establish a National AI Strategic Committee, to be convened by the Premier of the Executive Yuan and composed of scholars, experts, representatives from AI-related private organizations and industry, ministers without…
articleRecords.sourceLabel: National Science and Technology Council — official Traditional Chinese text

articleRecords.label: Article 7
articleRecords.title: Article 7
articleRecords.summary: To enhance the knowledge and skills of the public regarding AI, the government shall continuously promote education in artificial intelligence and ethics in schools at all levels, industries, organizations, society, and public agencies…
articleRecords.sourceLabel: National Science and Technology Council — official Traditional Chinese text

articleRecords.label: Article 8
articleRecords.title: Article 8
articleRecords.summary: The government shall implement AI development policies and encourage cross-disciplinary cooperation, exchange, and the establishment of infrastructure for talent and technology among government, industry, and academia.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: National Science and Technology Council — official Traditional Chinese text

articleRecords.label: Article 9
articleRecords.title: Article 9
articleRecords.summary: The government shall, within its financial capacity, make adequate budgetary allocations and take necessary measures to continuously ensure that funding meets the needs of promoting AI policy development.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: National Science and Technology Council — official Traditional Chinese text

articleRecords.label: Article 10
articleRecords.title: Article 10
articleRecords.summary: The government shall actively promote the R&D, application, and infrastructure of AI, properly plan the overall allocation of resources, and handle subsidies, commissions, funding, investments, incentives, and guidance for AI-related…
articleRecords.sourceLabel: National Science and Technology Council — official Traditional Chinese text

articleRecords.label: Article 11
articleRecords.title: Article 11
articleRecords.summary: When developing, training, testing, and verifying the operational impact of emerging AI technologies, the government shall provide for reasonable use, support, and subsidy measures, and shall improve the legal framework for AI research, development, and application.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: National Science and Technology Council — official Traditional Chinese text

articleRecords.label: Article 12
articleRecords.title: Article 12
articleRecords.summary: The government shall strive to promote international cooperation related to AI; and, based on the principle of public-private partnership, shall actively promote the innovative applications of AI in collaboration with the private sector.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: National Science and Technology Council — official Traditional Chinese text

articleRecords.label: Article 13
articleRecords.title: Article 13
articleRecords.summary: The government shall establish mechanisms for open data, data sharing, and data reuse to enhance the availability of data for AI, and shall regularly review and revise relevant laws and regulations.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: National Science and Technology Council — official Traditional Chinese text

articleRecords.label: Article 14
articleRecords.title: Article 14
articleRecords.summary: Each competent authority for the relevant industries shall consult with the competent authority for personal data protection, and shall, in the process of AI R&D and application, avoid the unnecessary collection, processing, or use of personal…
articleRecords.sourceLabel: National Science and Technology Council — official Traditional Chinese text

articleRecords.label: Article 15
articleRecords.title: Article 15
articleRecords.summary: The government shall actively use AI to safeguard workers' labor rights.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: National Science and Technology Council — official Traditional Chinese text

articleRecords.label: Article 16
articleRecords.title: Article 16
articleRecords.summary: The Ministry of Digital Affairs shall, with reference to international standards or norms, promote an AI risk taxonomy and assessment framework that is interoperable with international…
articleRecords.sourceLabel: National Science and Technology Council — official Traditional Chinese text

articleRecords.label: Article 17
articleRecords.title: Article 17
articleRecords.summary: For the application of high-risk AI, the government shall clarify the attribution of liability and the conditions for such liability, and shall establish relief, compensation, or insurance mechanisms.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: National Science and Technology Council — official Traditional Chinese text

articleRecords.label: Article 18
articleRecords.title: Article 18
articleRecords.summary: The government shall review the laws, regulations, and administrative measures under its jurisdiction in accordance with the provisions of this Act.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: National Science and Technology Council — official Traditional Chinese text

articleRecords.label: Article 19
articleRecords.title: Article 19
articleRecords.summary: When using AI to perform duties or provide services, the government shall conduct risk assessments and plan risk response measures.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: National Science and Technology Council — official Traditional Chinese text

articleRecords.label: Article 20
articleRecords.title: Article 20
articleRecords.summary: This Act shall take effect on the date of its promulgation.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: National Science and Technology Council — official Traditional Chinese text

articleRecords.summary: Article (28) The Executive Regulation The Council of Ministers, based upon a suggestion from the General Director of the Bureau, shall issue the Executive Regulations of this Decree by Law within six (6) months as of the date on which the Decree by Law is promulgated.

articleRecords.label: Article 1
articleRecords.title: Article 1
articleRecords.summary: Article 1 For the purpose of implementing this Law, the following terms shall have the meanings assigned thereto, unless the context requires otherwise: 1-Law: The Personal Data Protection Law.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: Saudi Data Governance Platform controlling Arabic text

articleRecords.label: Article 2
articleRecords.title: Article 2
articleRecords.summary: Article 2 1-The Law applies to any Processing of Personal Data related to individuals that takes place in the Kingdom by any means, including the Processing of Personal Data related to individuals residing in the Kingdom by any means from any party outside the Kingdom.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: Saudi Data Governance Platform controlling Arabic text

articleRecords.label: Article 3
articleRecords.title: Article 3
articleRecords.summary: Article 3 The provisions and procedures stated in this Law shall not prejudice any provision that grants a right to the Data Subject or confers better protection to Personal Data pursuant to any other law or an international agreement to which the Kingdom is a party.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: Saudi Data Governance Platform controlling Arabic text

articleRecords.label: Article 4
articleRecords.title: Article 4
articleRecords.summary: Article 4 Data Subject shall have the following rights pursuant to this Law and as set out in the Regulations: 1-The right to be informed about the legal basis and the purpose of the Collection of their Personal Data.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: Saudi Data Governance Platform controlling Arabic text

articleRecords.label: Article 5
articleRecords.title: Article 5
articleRecords.summary: Article 5 1-Except for the cases stated in this Law, neither Personal Data may be processed nor the purpose of Personal Data Processing may be changed without the consent of the Data Subject.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: Saudi Data Governance Platform controlling Arabic text

articleRecords.label: Article 6
articleRecords.title: Article 6
articleRecords.summary: Article 6 In the following cases, Processing of Personal Data shall not be subject to the consent referred to in Paragraph (1) of Article (5) herein: 1-If the Processing serves actual interests of the Data…
articleRecords.sourceLabel: Saudi Data Governance Platform controlling Arabic text

articleRecords.label: Article 7
articleRecords.title: Article 7
articleRecords.summary: Article 7 The consent referred to in paragraph (1) of Article (5) of this Law may not form a condition of providing a service or a benefit, unless such service or benefit is directly related to the Processing of Personal Data for which the consent is given.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: Saudi Data Governance Platform controlling Arabic text

articleRecords.label: Article 8
articleRecords.title: Article 8
articleRecords.summary: Article 8 Subject to the provisions of this Law and the Regulations regarding the Disclosure of Personal Data, the Controller shall only select Processors providing the necessary guarantees to implement the provisions of this Law and the Regulations.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: Saudi Data Governance Platform controlling Arabic text

articleRecords.label: Article 9
articleRecords.title: Article 9
articleRecords.summary: Article 9 1-The Controller may set time frames for exercising the right to access Personal Data stated in Paragraph (2) of Article (4) herein as stipulated in the Regulations.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: Saudi Data Governance Platform controlling Arabic text

articleRecords.label: Article 10
articleRecords.title: Article 10
articleRecords.summary: Article 10 The Controller may only collect Personal Data directly from the Data Subject and may only process Personal Data for the purposes for which they have been collected.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: Saudi Data Governance Platform controlling Arabic text

articleRecords.label: Article 11
articleRecords.title: Article 11
articleRecords.summary: Article 11 1- The purpose for which Personal Data is collected shall be directly related to the Controller’s purposes, and shall not contravene any legal provisions.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: Saudi Data Governance Platform controlling Arabic text

articleRecords.label: Article 12
articleRecords.title: Article 12
articleRecords.summary: Article 12 The Controller shall use a privacy policy and make it available to Data Subjects for their information prior to collecting their Personal Data.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: Saudi Data Governance Platform controlling Arabic text

articleRecords.label: Article 13
articleRecords.title: Article 13
articleRecords.summary: Article 13 When collecting Personal Data directly from the Data Subject, the Controller shall take appropriate measures to inform the Data Subject of the following upon Collection: 1- The legal basis for collecting their Personal Data.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: Saudi Data Governance Platform controlling Arabic text

articleRecords.label: Article 14
articleRecords.title: Article 14
articleRecords.summary: Article 14 The Controller may not process Personal Data without taking sufficient steps to verify the Personal Data accuracy, completeness, timeliness and relevance to the purpose for which it is collected in accordance with the provisions of the Law.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: Saudi Data Governance Platform controlling Arabic text

articleRecords.label: Article 15
articleRecords.title: Article 15
articleRecords.summary: Article 15 The Controller may not Disclose Personal Data except in the following situations: 1- Data Subject consents to the Disclosure in accordance with the provisions of the Law.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: Saudi Data Governance Platform controlling Arabic text

articleRecords.label: Article 16
articleRecords.title: Article 16
articleRecords.summary: Article 16 The Controller shall not disclose Personal Data in the situations stated in Paragraphs (1, 2, 5) and (6) of Article (15) if the Disclosure: 1- Represents a threat to security, harms the reputation of the Kingdom, or conflicts with the interests of the Kingdom.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: Saudi Data Governance Platform controlling Arabic text

articleRecords.label: Article 17
articleRecords.title: Article 17
articleRecords.summary: Article 17 1- If Personal Data is corrected, completed or updated, the Controller shall notify such amendment to all the other entities to which such Personal Data has been transferred and make the amendment available to such entities.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: Saudi Data Governance Platform controlling Arabic text

articleRecords.label: Article 18
articleRecords.title: Article 18
articleRecords.summary: Article 18 1- The Controller shall, without undue delay, Destroy the Personal Data when no longer necessary for the purpose for which they were collected.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: Saudi Data Governance Platform controlling Arabic text

articleRecords.label: Article 19
articleRecords.title: Article 19
articleRecords.summary: Article 19 The Controller shall implement all the necessary organizational, administrative and technical measures to protect Personal Data, including during the Transfer of Personal Data, in accordance with the provisions and controls set out in the Regulations.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: Saudi Data Governance Platform controlling Arabic text

articleRecords.label: Article 20
articleRecords.title: Article 20
articleRecords.summary: Article 20 1- The Controller shall notify the Competent Authority upon knowing of any breach, damage, or illegal access to personal data, in accordance with the Regulations.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: Saudi Data Governance Platform controlling Arabic text

articleRecords.label: Article 21
articleRecords.title: Article 21
articleRecords.summary: Article 21 The Controller shall respond to the requests of the Data Subject pertaining to their rights under this Law within such period and in such method as set out in the Regulations.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: Saudi Data Governance Platform controlling Arabic text

articleRecords.label: Article 22
articleRecords.title: Article 22
articleRecords.summary: Article 22 The Controller shall conduct an impact assessment of Personal Data Processing in relation to any product or service, based on the nature of the activity carried out by the Controller, in accordance with the relevant provisions of the Regulations.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: Saudi Data Governance Platform controlling Arabic text

articleRecords.label: Article 23
articleRecords.title: Article 23
articleRecords.summary: Article 23 Without prejudice to this Law, the Regulations shall set out additional controls and procedures for the Processing of Health Data in a manner that ensures the privacy of the Data Subject and protects their rights under this Law.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: Saudi Data Governance Platform controlling Arabic text

articleRecords.label: Article 24
articleRecords.title: Article 24
articleRecords.summary: Article 24 Without prejudice to this Law, the Regulations shall set out additional controls and procedures for the Processing of Credit Data in a manner that ensures the privacy of the Data Subject and protects their rights under this Law and the Credit Information Law.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: Saudi Data Governance Platform controlling Arabic text

articleRecords.label: Article 25
articleRecords.title: Article 25
articleRecords.summary: Article 25 With the exception of the awareness-raising materials sent by Public Entities, Controller may not use personal means of communication, including the post and email, of the Data Subject to send advertising or awareness-raising…
articleRecords.sourceLabel: Saudi Data Governance Platform controlling Arabic text

articleRecords.label: Article 26
articleRecords.title: Article 26
articleRecords.summary: Article 26 With the exception of Sensitive Data, Personal Data may be processed for marketing purposes, if it is collected directly from the Data Subject and their consent is given in accordance with the provisions of…
articleRecords.sourceLabel: Saudi Data Governance Platform controlling Arabic text

articleRecords.label: Article 27
articleRecords.title: Article 27
articleRecords.summary: Article 27 Personal data may be collected or processed for scientific, research, or statistical purposes without the consent of the Data Subject in the following situations: 1-If it does not specifically identify the Data Subject.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: Saudi Data Governance Platform controlling Arabic text

articleRecords.label: Article 28
articleRecords.title: Article 28
articleRecords.summary: Article 28 It is not permissible to copy official documents where Data Subjects are identifiable, except where it is required by law, or when a competent public authority requests copying such documents pursuant to the Regulations.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: Saudi Data Governance Platform controlling Arabic text

articleRecords.label: Article 29
articleRecords.title: Article 29

articleRecords.summary: Article 2: Personal or family use 1- The provisions of the Law and its Regulations shall not apply to an individual Processing Personal Data for purposes not exceeding personal or family use.

articleRecords.summary: Article 12: Consent withdrawal 1- A Data Subject shall have the right to withdraw their consent for Processing their Personal Data at any time, and they shall inform the Controller of this through any available mean in accordance with Article (4) of this Regulation.

articleRecords.summary: Article 14: Processing to serve Actual Interest of Data Subject When processing data to serve Actual Interest of the Data Subject, the Controller shall retain evidence that such interest exists and that it is not possible to contact or communicate with the Data Subject.

articleRecords.summary: Article 16: Processing for Legitimate Interest 1- Except Public Entities, a Controller may process Personal Data to achieve a Legitimate Interest provided that the following conditions are met: a) Purpose shall not violate any of the laws in the Kingdom.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: Saudi Data Governance Platform controlling Arabic text

articleRecords.title: Notification of a Purpose of Use when Acquiring Personal Information
articleRecords.originalTitle: 第二十一条（取得に際しての利用目的の通知等）
articleRecords.summary: Article 21 (1)Unless the purpose of use has already been disclosed to the public, a business handling personal information must promptly notify the identifiable person of that purpose of use or disclose this to the public once it has acquired personal information.

articleRecords.label: Article 22
articleRecords.title: Maintaining the Accuracy of Data
articleRecords.originalTitle: 第二十二条（データ内容の正確性の確保等）
articleRecords.summary: Article 22 Businesses handling personal information must endeavor to keep the content of personal data accurate and up to date, within the scope necessary for achieving the purpose of use, and delete the personal data without delay if they no longer require it.

articleRecords.summary: Article 23 Businesses handling personal information must take the necessary and appropriate measures for managing the security of personal data including preventing the leaking, loss or damage of the personal data they handle.

articleRecords.label: Art. 7

articleRecords.summary: Requires controllers to embed appropriate technical and organisational data-protection measures from the planning stage and to use defaults that limit processing to the minimum needed for the intended purpose.

articleRecords.summary: Requires controllers and processors to use risk-appropriate technical and organisational measures to secure personal data and prevent breaches.

articleRecords.summary: Permits disclosure abroad on the basis of an adequacy decision or, in its absence, specified safeguards such as treaties, clauses, standard clauses or binding corporate rules.

articleRecords.label: Art. 21

articleRecords.summary: Requires notice of qualifying solely automated individual decisions and normally provides an opportunity to state a position and obtain review by a natural person.

chapter.label: Chapter 3
chapter.title: Duties of the Controller and of the Processor

articleRecords.label: Art. 22
articleRecords.title: Data protection impact assessment
articleRecords.summary: Requires a prior data protection impact assessment where planned processing is likely to create a high risk, including specified uses of new technologies and large-scale sensitive-data processing.

chapter.label: Chapter 3
chapter.title: Duties of the Controller and of the Processor

articleRecords.label: Art. 23
articleRecords.title: Consultation of the FDPIC
articleRecords.summary: Requires prior consultation with the FDPIC when high residual risk remains after the measures identified through the impact assessment, subject to the statutory data-protection-officer route.

chapter.title: Duties of the Controller and of the Processor

articleRecords.label: Art. 24
articleRecords.title: Notifications of data security breaches
articleRecords.summary: Requires rapid notification of qualifying high-risk data security breaches, processor-to-controller notice and, where required, notice to affected individuals; paragraph 5bis has applied since 1 April 2025.

articleRecords.label: Article 1
articleRecords.title: Purpose
articleRecords.summary: Article 1 (Purpose) The purpose of this Act is to protect the freedom and rights of individuals, and further, to realize the dignity and value of the individuals, by prescribing the processing and protection of personal information.

articleRecords.label: Preamble
articleRecords.title: Enacting authority
articleRecords.summary: States the constitutional and statutory authority for the former executive-branch AI directives.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: GovInfo — official electronic Federal Register PDF

articleRecords.label: Section 1
articleRecords.title: Purpose
articleRecords.summary: Frames AI as a source of major benefits and risks and calls for coordinated, society-wide governance.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: GovInfo — official electronic Federal Register PDF

articleRecords.label: Section 2
articleRecords.title: Policy and Principles
articleRecords.summary: Sets eight policy principles covering safety, innovation, workers, civil rights, consumers, privacy, federal use, and international leadership.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: GovInfo — official electronic Federal Register PDF

articleRecords.label: Section 3
articleRecords.title: Definitions
articleRecords.summary: Defines the technical, security, privacy, infrastructure, and institutional terms used throughout the Order.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: GovInfo — official electronic Federal Register PDF

articleRecords.label: Section 4
articleRecords.title: Ensuring the Safety and Security of AI Technology
articleRecords.summary: Organizes the Order's former AI safety and security program.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: GovInfo — official electronic Federal Register PDF

articleRecords.label: Section 4.1
articleRecords.title: Developing Guidelines, Standards, and Best Practices for AI Safety and Security
articleRecords.summary: Directed NIST and partner agencies to develop evaluation, red-teaming, secure-development, testbed, and risk-management guidance.

chapter.label: Section 4
chapter.title: Ensuring the Safety and Security of AI Technology

articleRecords.sourceLabel: GovInfo — official electronic Federal Register PDF

articleRecords.label: Section 4.2
articleRecords.title: Ensuring Safe and Reliable AI
articleRecords.summary: Directed reporting for specified dual-use foundation models and large computing clusters and set interim compute thresholds.

chapter.label: Section 4
chapter.title: Ensuring the Safety and Security of AI Technology

articleRecords.sourceLabel: GovInfo — official electronic Federal Register PDF

articleRecords.label: Section 4.3
articleRecords.title: Managing AI in Critical Infrastructure and in Cybersecurity
articleRecords.summary: Directed critical-infrastructure, cloud, and cybersecurity risk-management measures for AI systems.

chapter.label: Section 4
chapter.title: Ensuring the Safety and Security of AI Technology

articleRecords.sourceLabel: GovInfo — official electronic Federal Register PDF

articleRecords.label: Section 4.4
articleRecords.title: Reducing Risks at the Intersection of AI and CBRN Threats
articleRecords.summary: Addressed chemical, biological, radiological, nuclear, and synthetic-biology risks associated with advanced AI.

chapter.label: Section 4
chapter.title: Ensuring the Safety and Security of AI Technology

articleRecords.sourceLabel: GovInfo — official electronic Federal Register PDF

articleRecords.label: Section 4.5
articleRecords.title: Reducing the Risks Posed by Synthetic Content
articleRecords.summary: Directed provenance, watermarking, authentication, and detection work for synthetic content.

chapter.label: Section 4
chapter.title: Ensuring the Safety and Security of AI Technology

articleRecords.sourceLabel: GovInfo — official electronic Federal Register PDF

articleRecords.label: Section 4.6
articleRecords.title: Soliciting Input on Dual-Use Foundation Models with Widely Available Model Weights
articleRecords.summary: Sought public input on risks and benefits of dual-use foundation models with widely available weights.

chapter.label: Section 4
chapter.title: Ensuring the Safety and Security of AI Technology

articleRecords.sourceLabel: GovInfo — official electronic Federal Register PDF

articleRecords.label: Section 4.7
articleRecords.title: Promoting Safe Release and Preventing the Malicious Use of Federal Data for AI Training
articleRecords.summary: Directed safeguards against unsafe or privacy-invasive use of federal data in AI training.

chapter.label: Section 4
chapter.title: Ensuring the Safety and Security of AI Technology

articleRecords.sourceLabel: GovInfo — official electronic Federal Register PDF

articleRecords.label: Section 4.8
articleRecords.title: Directing the Development of a National Security Memorandum
articleRecords.summary: Directed a national-security memorandum on AI governance, security, and use within national-security systems.

chapter.label: Section 4
chapter.title: Ensuring the Safety and Security of AI Technology

articleRecords.sourceLabel: GovInfo — official electronic Federal Register PDF

articleRecords.label: Section 5
articleRecords.title: Promoting Innovation and Competition
articleRecords.summary: Organizes the former talent, innovation, research, intellectual-property, and competition measures.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: GovInfo — official electronic Federal Register PDF

articleRecords.label: Section 5.1
articleRecords.title: Attracting AI Talent to the United States
articleRecords.summary: Directed immigration and talent-attraction measures for AI and other critical technologies.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: GovInfo — official electronic Federal Register PDF

articleRecords.label: Section 5.2
articleRecords.title: Promoting Innovation
articleRecords.summary: Directed research infrastructure, grants, small-business support, patent, copyright, and competition-related AI measures.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: GovInfo — official electronic Federal Register PDF

articleRecords.label: Section 5.3
articleRecords.title: Promoting Competition
articleRecords.summary: Directed competition analysis and action concerning compute, data, cloud, semiconductors, and AI markets.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: GovInfo — official electronic Federal Register PDF

articleRecords.label: Section 6
articleRecords.title: Supporting Workers
articleRecords.summary: Directed study and mitigation of AI's effects on workers, job quality, labor standards, and workforce development.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: GovInfo — official electronic Federal Register PDF

articleRecords.label: Section 7
articleRecords.title: Advancing Equity and Civil Rights
articleRecords.summary: Organizes the former civil-rights and equity directives for justice, benefits, hiring, housing, and other consequential domains.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: GovInfo — official electronic Federal Register PDF

articleRecords.label: Section 7.1
articleRecords.title: Strengthening AI and Civil Rights in the Criminal Justice System
articleRecords.summary: Directed safeguards, evaluation, and reporting for criminal-justice and law-enforcement uses of AI.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: GovInfo — official electronic Federal Register PDF

articleRecords.label: Section 7.2
articleRecords.title: Protecting Civil Rights Related to Government Benefits and Programs
articleRecords.summary: Directed agency guidance to prevent unlawful discrimination in public-benefit administration involving algorithms.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: GovInfo — official electronic Federal Register PDF

articleRecords.label: Section 7.3
articleRecords.title: Strengthening AI and Civil Rights in the Broader Economy
articleRecords.summary: Directed civil-rights enforcement and guidance for employment, housing, and other private-sector AI uses.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: GovInfo — official electronic Federal Register PDF

articleRecords.label: Section 8
articleRecords.title: Protecting Consumers, Patients, Passengers, and Students
articleRecords.summary: Directed consumer, healthcare, transportation, education, communications, and critical-service AI safeguards.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: GovInfo — official electronic Federal Register PDF

articleRecords.label: Section 9
articleRecords.title: Protecting Privacy
articleRecords.summary: Directed privacy research, privacy-enhancing technologies, data safeguards, and evaluation of commercially available information.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: GovInfo — official electronic Federal Register PDF

articleRecords.label: Section 10
articleRecords.title: Advancing Federal Government Use of AI
articleRecords.summary: Organizes the former federal AI governance, procurement, inventory, workforce, and responsible-use program.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: GovInfo — official electronic Federal Register PDF

articleRecords.label: Section 10.1
articleRecords.title: Providing Guidance for AI Management
articleRecords.summary: Directed OMB guidance for agency AI governance, impact management, procurement, inventories, and rights-impacting uses.

chapter.label: Section 10
chapter.title: Advancing Federal Government Use of AI

articleRecords.sourceLabel: GovInfo — official electronic Federal Register PDF

articleRecords.label: Section 10.2
articleRecords.title: Increasing AI Talent in Government
articleRecords.summary: Directed federal recruitment, training, and capacity-building for AI expertise.

chapter.label: Section 10
chapter.title: Advancing Federal Government Use of AI

articleRecords.sourceLabel: GovInfo — official electronic Federal Register PDF

articleRecords.label: Section 11
articleRecords.title: Strengthening American Leadership Abroad
articleRecords.summary: Directed international engagement on common AI safety, standards, and responsible-development approaches.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: GovInfo — official electronic Federal Register PDF

articleRecords.label: Section 12
articleRecords.title: Implementation
articleRecords.summary: Assigned interagency implementation, coordination, deadline, and reporting responsibilities.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: GovInfo — official electronic Federal Register PDF

articleRecords.title: General Provisions
articleRecords.summary: Preserves agency authorities, applicable-law constraints, appropriations limits, and the absence of a private enforceable right.

articleRecords.label: Preamble
articleRecords.title: Enacting authority
articleRecords.summary: States the constitutional and statutory authority for the current executive-branch AI policy order.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: GovInfo — official electronic Federal Register PDF

articleRecords.label: Section 1
articleRecords.title: Purpose
articleRecords.summary: States the Order's rationale: removing identified policy barriers to US AI leadership.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: GovInfo — official electronic Federal Register PDF

articleRecords.label: Section 2
articleRecords.title: Policy
articleRecords.summary: Sets the policy of sustaining and enhancing US global AI dominance for human flourishing, competitiveness, and national security.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: GovInfo — official electronic Federal Register PDF

articleRecords.label: Section 3
articleRecords.title: Definition
articleRecords.summary: Incorporates the statutory definition of artificial intelligence in 15 U.S.C. 9401(3).
articleRecords.sourceLabel: GovInfo — official electronic Federal Register PDF

articleRecords.label: Section 4
articleRecords.title: Developing an Artificial Intelligence Action Plan
articleRecords.summary: Directed preparation of a presidential AI Action Plan within 180 days.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: GovInfo — official electronic Federal Register PDF

articleRecords.label: Section 5
articleRecords.title: Implementation of Order Revocation
articleRecords.summary: Directed review and possible suspension, revision, or rescission of actions taken under revoked EO 14110 and revision of two OMB memoranda.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: GovInfo — official electronic Federal Register PDF

articleRecords.label: Section 6
articleRecords.title: General Provisions
articleRecords.summary: Preserves agency authorities, applicable-law and appropriations limits, and the absence of a private enforceable right.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: GovInfo — official electronic Federal Register PDF

articleRecords.label: 總說明
articleRecords.title: 制定背景與治理原則
articleRecords.summary: 說明生成式AI帶來的行政效率與資訊、權利及機密風險，確立負責任、可信賴、安全、隱私、資料治理及問責等總體原則，並要求保留人員的自主權與控制權。
articleRecords.sourceLabel: National Science and Technology Council — official Traditional-Chinese ODT

articleRecords.label: 第一點
articleRecords.title: 目的與風險範圍
articleRecords.summary: 以提升行政效率為目的，並將國家安全、資訊安全、人權、隱私、倫理及法律風險列為各機關使用生成式AI時的基本治理範圍。
articleRecords.sourceLabel: National Science and Technology Council — official Traditional-Chinese ODT

articleRecords.label: 第二點
articleRecords.title: 人工最終判斷與自主性
articleRecords.summary: 生成資訊不得取代承辦人的客觀專業最終判斷、自主思維、創造力與人際互動。
articleRecords.sourceLabel: National Science and Technology Council — official Traditional-Chinese ODT

articleRecords.label: 第三點
articleRecords.title: 機密文書禁用生成式AI
articleRecords.summary: 國家機密與一般公務機密文書必須由承辦人親自撰寫，禁止使用生成式AI製作。
articleRecords.sourceLabel: National Science and Technology Council — official Traditional-Chinese ODT

articleRecords.label: 第四點
articleRecords.title: 機密與個人資料輸入限制
articleRecords.summary: 不得向生成式AI提供或詢問涉密、公務未公開或個人資料；封閉式地端部署在確認環境安全後，可依文書或資訊機密等級分級使用。
articleRecords.sourceLabel: National Science and Technology Council — official Traditional-Chinese ODT

articleRecords.label: 第五點
articleRecords.title: 產出查核與公務決策限制
articleRecords.summary: 機關不得完全信任未經確認的生成內容，也不得直接據此作成行政行為或將其作為公務決策的唯一依據。
articleRecords.sourceLabel: National Science and Technology Council — official Traditional-Chinese ODT

articleRecords.label: 第六點
articleRecords.title: 生成式AI輔助使用揭露
articleRecords.summary: 各機關以生成式AI輔助執行業務或提供服務時，應作適當揭露。
articleRecords.sourceLabel: National Science and Technology Council — official Traditional-Chinese ODT

articleRecords.label: 第七點
articleRecords.title: 法規遵循、權利保護與內控
articleRecords.summary: 使用生成式AI須遵守資通安全、個資、著作權及資訊使用規定，注意智慧財產權與人格權風險；機關可依設備及業務制定規範或內控。
articleRecords.sourceLabel: National Science and Technology Council — official Traditional-Chinese ODT

articleRecords.label: 第八點
articleRecords.title: 採購與供應商治理
articleRecords.summary: 機關辦理採購時，應要求得標者注意本指引，並遵守機關依第七點制定的規範或內控措施。
articleRecords.sourceLabel: National Science and Technology Council — official Traditional-Chinese ODT

articleRecords.label: 第九點
articleRecords.title: 公營與受政府支持機構準用
articleRecords.summary: 公營事業、公立學校、行政法人及政府捐助的財團法人得準用本指引。
articleRecords.sourceLabel: National Science and Technology Council — official Traditional-Chinese ODT

articleRecords.label: 第十點
articleRecords.title: 其他機關參照
articleRecords.summary: 行政院及其所屬機關（構）以外的機關，得參照本指引制定自身的生成式AI使用規範。
articleRecords.sourceLabel: National Science and Technology Council — official Traditional-Chinese ODT

articleRecords.summary: Requires deployers to retain compliance records for at least three years after a consequential decision.

articleRecords.label: C.R.S. § 6-1-1704

articleRecords.summary: Requires point-of-interaction notice and post-adverse-outcome explanations and directs rulemaking on disclosure content.

articleRecords.label: C.R.S. § 6-1-1705
articleRecords.title: Consumer rights — correction, human review, and reconsideration
articleRecords.summary: Gives consumers data-access, correction, meaningful-human-review, and reconsideration pathways after specified adverse outcomes.

articleRecords.summary: Establishes Attorney General enforcement, a temporary cure process, reporting, stakeholder rulemaking, and procedural safeguards.
articleRecords.sourceLabel: Colorado Session Laws 2026, chapter 131 — official PDF

articleRecords.title: Liability, fault allocation, and indemnification
articleRecords.summary: Allocates developer and deployer fault in existing discrimination actions and limits indemnification for each party's own conduct.
